mechanism_narrative: A persistently lower FEDFUNDS rate raises equity valuations by reducing the discount rate on future earnings, so a decline in policy rates should precede positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or the signal's Rank IC stays below 0.03 in rolling 2-year windows, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS rises above its recent trend, it signals stronger household and business spending, which lifts near-term corporate revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth does not correspond to above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered observations produce negative median returns over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates weakening labor income and deteriorating demand, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations weaken.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment shocks fail to precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the median return after the trigger is non-negative across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Falling INDPRO with rising HOUST identifies a demand-supply mismatch in which industrial production weakness dominates, signaling slower aggregate activity and negative S&P 500 returns over roughly one quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint state of declining INDPRO and soft HOUST does not produce negative 60-day forward SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real rates tighten and multiple compression dominates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: Reject if episodes where inflation exceeds the policy rate do not precede weaker 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction does not outperform a model using CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent PAYEMS growth confirms ongoing payroll expansion and stable income formation, which supports equity demand and should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained payroll gains fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relation reverses in periods with stable unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST after a prior peak signals a housing demand rollover, which weakens wealth effects and slows consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% from a 6-month high
failure_condition: Reject if housing starts drawdowns do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the median post-trigger return is not below the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: Strong INDPRO combined with rising PAYEMS marks a broad production and hiring upswing, which lifts earnings expectations and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint strength of industrial production and payrolls does not improve 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction coefficient is unstable and statistically insignificant over rolling 3-year windows.
